report_filename_note: Written to *-recheck.json fallback per spec instruction. Original *-20260519-211515.json (pre-fix snapshot) preserved for diff traceability; write-guard blocks overwrite of existing reports.
re_audit_trigger: Standard 6 fix in commands/commit.md:130 — replaced inline Chinese phrase with English paraphrase + parenthetical attribution to docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md
prior_verdict_reference: docs/dev/prompt-inspector-report-20260519-211515.json (pre-fix snapshot: PASS, 0 critical / 0 major / 0 minor, 4 advisory_observations sub-threshold)
scope_contract: cleanliness-of-THIS-diff only; emits new-in-this-diff findings tagged via Mechanism (ii) inspector-side tagging with explicit introduced_in_diff field on every finding
out_of_scope_reason: Prompt-inspector scope is .claude/commands/*.md and .claude/agents/*.md only. CLAUDE.md is global config (standing instructions, not command/agent execution doc). Policies, hooks, tests are non-markdown (.py/.sh/.json) — out of inspector scope; cleanliness for those belongs to cleanliness-inspector / qa.

re_audit_focused_delta.diff_summary: Single-line in-place replacement. -1 / +1. Net line delta = 0. No new ## headers; no new sections; no list expansion; no template insertion. Inside existing ## Step-by-step workflow > Step 7 procedural section.
re_audit_focused_delta.removed_text_summary: Inline Chinese binding phrase at end of Step 7 algorithm preamble paragraph
re_audit_focused_delta.added_text_summary: English paraphrase of the binding directive plus a parenthetical attribution clause pointing to docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md where the Chinese phrase is preserved verbatim under Standard 6 exemption
re_audit_focused_delta.rule_match: none — does not match any prompt-inspector verbose-section detection target (## Philosophy / ## Overview / ## Quality Standards / ## Safety Features / ## Helper Scripts / ## Usage>50 / ## Examples>50). Does not match the 'markdown template in execution steps >30 lines' warning pattern. Does not match repeated-explanation-across-steps pattern.
re_audit_focused_delta.verbose_lines_added_rationale: Per prompt-inspector severity calculation, verbose_lines counts lines in detected verbose SECTIONS, not character-count of inline clauses inside legitimate procedural rules. Section detection requires a ## header match. The replaced clause sits in an existing rule paragraph and does not introduce or extend any verbose-section header.

diff_insertion_summary_cumulative_for_cy.note: Cumulative cycle insertions are unchanged from the pre-fix snapshot; the Std 6 fix is net-zero. Recorded here for traceability.

Reference / cross-doc note, not a story section

advisory_observations.description: The parenthetical at line 130 is slightly wordy but is attribution for WHY the Chinese phrase moved out-of-line, not narrative/story content. It is a single inline clause inside an existing rule paragraph; it does not introduce a new section, does not match any prompt-inspector verbose-section target, and does not approach the 50-line minor threshold. Codex confirmed this is OBSERVATION_ONLY, not a flagged violation.
advisory_observations.codex_optional_tighter_wording: If the user later wants tighter phrasing, codex offered (OPTIONAL ONLY, not required): 'source phrase preserved in docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md under Standard 6 exemption' — entirely optional, not required.
advisory_observations.reason_non_blocking: Net line delta = 0; severity = none under prompt-inspector rules (file-level/section-level granularity). Single-clause copyediting is outside inspector scope per codex CF re-audit.

severity_threshold_application.binding_per_codex_CF4_prior: absolute file-level thresholds bind; aggregated multi-file insertions do NOT promote individual sub-threshold blocks to findings

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex re-audit confirmed the prior PASS holds. The Std 6 fix is a single inline clause at commit.md:130 with net-zero line delta; does not match any prompt-inspector verbose-section target; does not approach the 50-line minor threshold. Codex offered three OBSERVATION_ONLY items (no PROPOSED_FIX blockers). Codex explicitly stated flagging this as minor/major/critical 'would be scope drift because the prompt-inspector rules are file-level/section-level verbosity checks, not single-clause copyediting.'

findings.issue: No severity issue: flagging this as minor/major/critical would be scope drift because the prompt-inspector rules are file-level/section-level verbosity checks, not single-clause copyediting
